name: Great-Grandmother Lillie's Turnip Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Gently scrub the turnips under cold running water. Trim off the root ends and green tops. Young turnips do not need peeling, but may be peeled.
2. Cut turnips into slices about a quarter of an inch thick. Set aside.
3. Rinse salt pork and place in a 3-quart soup kettle with water. Turn heat to medium-low and bring water slowly to just below boiling point, carefully skimming off scum as it rises.
4. Lower heat to a bare simmer and cook pork gently for about an hour.
5. Raise heat to medium-high and add sliced turnips. Let liquid come back to boil, skim again, then reduce heat to low. Simmer until turnips are tender (20-30 minutes). Discard pork and skim excess fat from broth.
6. Remove turnips with a slotted spoon. Cube a cup of them and grind remainder through colander or food mill. Return both to broth. Bring soup back to simmer, taste, correct salt, and add black pepper.
7. With hand, stir cornmeal into soup constantly until it falls in thin stream between fingers without stopping. Simmer until thickened (about 5 minutes).
8. If desired, stir in optional cream for about two more minutes before serving.
